idea-plugin.name: GenAI PHP Portable
idea-plugin.description:
Write & run PHP code in IntelliJ IDEA Community for free. PHP Portable is a lightweight, feature-rich way to add PHP support to the free IDE — no PhpStorm license and no system-wide PHP install required.

Minimal PHP support for IntelliJ IDEA Community (which does not bundle the paid PhpStorm language plugin):
  • Portable PHP SDK — download a portable PHP interpreter from inside the IDE (like the JDK downloader); no system install required.
  • Run PHP script — a run configuration that executes a .php file with the selected PHP SDK or an explicit interpreter path.
  • Syntax highlighting — lightweight, lexer-based highlighting for .php files (tags, keywords, strings, comments, variables, numbers).
This is an MVP; semantic features (completion, go-to-def, inspections) are a later step, best delivered by wrapping a PHP language server via LSP4IJ.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.1.2
  • Maintenance: replaced an internal-only platform API with its public equivalent and updated deprecated icon-loading calls, for forward compatibility with newer IDE builds. No functional changes.
0.1.1
  • Works alongside PhpStorm & IDEA Ultimate. No longer clashes with the bundled PHP plugin: the editor language support quietly steps aside where PHP is already supported, while the portable interpreter, run action, and setup tools keep working in every IDE.
  • Batteries-included PHP. Downloaded interpreters now bundle ~65 extensions (intl, gd, soap, curl, zip, sodium, mbstring, and more), so composer install and real-world projects run without hitting a missing-extension error.
  • Clearer run action. The run configuration is now named "PHP Script (Portable)" so it's easy to tell apart from PhpStorm's built-in "PHP Script".
  • Clearer settings. The interpreter settings page is now Settings ▸ Languages & Frameworks ▸ Portable PHP, so it no longer looks like a duplicate of PhpStorm's own PHP settings.
